retry_reason: Put operations are final; retrying would cause double-release or use-after-free.
contract_summary: This function releases resources held by an i915_vma_work: it decrements the refcount on the gem object (if non-NULL) via i915_gem_object_put, decrements the refcount on the vma resource via i915_vma_resource_put, and frees any page table stash via i915_vm_free_pt_stash. After calling this function, the caller must not use the vw->obj or vw->vma_res pointers, as they may have been freed. This is typically called when the associated async DMA fence work completes.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is release of refcounted objects (gem object, vma resource) and freeing of page table stash. The function is a composite release wrapper with two put operations and one memory free.
judge.retry_reason: Put operations are final; retrying would cause double-release or use-after-free.
judge.contract_summary: This function releases resources held by an i915_vma_work: it decrements the refcount on the gem object (if non-NULL) via i915_gem_object_put, decrements the refcount on the vma resource via i915_vma_resource_put, and frees any page table stash via i915_vm_free_pt_stash. After calling this function, the caller must not use the vw->obj or vw->vma_res pointers, as they may have been freed. This is typically called when the associated async DMA fence work completes.

check.reason: Both candidate operations use access paths starting from 'work' (struct dma_fence_work *) to reach fields 'obj' and 'vma_res', but these fields are members of the containing struct i915_vma_work, not of dma_fence_work. The function obtains vw via container_of, so the actual critical variables passed to the put functions are vw->obj and vw->vma_res. The provided paths 'work.obj...' and 'work.vma_res...' are structurally invalid because 'work' does not have those fields. Thus the extractions do not accurately represent the code. All operations are rejected.

reevaluate.reason: The function is a release wrapper that calls i915_gem_object_put and i915_vma_resource_put on fields of the containing i915_vma_work struct, reached via container_of from the work parameter. The candidate operations correctly identify the critical variables and refcount operations; the path representation is a simplification but the underlying semantics are correct. The check stage's rejection based on structural path validity is overruled because the container_of relationship makes the fields reachable from work.

final.retry_reason: Put operations are final; retrying would cause double-release or use-after-free.

After calling this function, the caller must not use the vw->obj or vw->vma_res pointers, as they may have been freed. This is typically called when the associated async DMA fence work completes.
